era: 1964-1966. Blues/Rock. E=7 -- the formation. Central District origin. Chitlin' Circuit. Sideman years. Pre-Experience.

circuit_employers.name: Curtis Knight and the Squires
circuit_employers.lesson: New York scene; PPX contract dispute

circuit_employers.name: Jimmy James and the Blue Flames
circuit_employers.lesson: First headlining role; Cafe Wha? discovery by Chas Chandler

army.partner_met: Billy Cox (bassist; Band of Gypsys, final touring period)

ppx_contract.consequence: Legal dispute after Experience success; Band of Gypsys album delivered as settlement to Capitol Records

theory_nodes.concept: The Chitlin' Circuit as infrastructure of Black American music during segregation. Hendrix's years on the circuit document both the formation of the musician and the history of the circuit itself -- Green Book venues, Black-owned establishments, economic structures sustaining Black artists.
theory_nodes.description: The Chitlin' Circuit as historical subject.

theory_nodes.concept: Left-hand constraint as innovation source begins here. Playing upside-down, restrung -- the chord voicings that standard technique could not provide forced new solutions. The innovation was born in the early Seattle years.
theory_nodes.description: Technical innovation rooted in physical constraint.

theory_nodes.concept: E=7 to E=8 threshold: the London crossing. The early career is complete in technique but incomplete in context. London provided the audience, band, studio resources, and cultural permission to push further. E=7 is the formation; E=8 is the expression.
theory_nodes.description: The E=7/8 split as documentary of artistic development.

theory_nodes.concept: PPX contract: $1 and a royalty. Systematic exploitation of young Black musicians in the music industry, documented. The contract shaped the Band of Gypsys album release -- the most political Hendrix recording -- as legal settlement.
theory_nodes.description: Music industry exploitation documented in the engine.

cross_references.relationship: Same artist. E=8. What the formation produced. London, the Experience, Woodstock. The engine's ceiling.
E=8. The grunge maximum follows the Hendrix sequence. 245-247 is the engine's greatest energy corridor.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=7 + E=7 = E=14. Snoqualmie Falls: 268 feet of roaring white-noise. Sacred to the Snoqualmie Tribe. Raw geological power pairs with raw musical formation -- both overwhelming forces not yet in their final form.
